title: Angst
description: As soon as the telephone thrummed, I knew. I was in the back bedroom. It was the last room in the world. At that time, the stories that came were telling me what they wanted. I could believe anything, for I’d stopped believing myself. Lying there, defenceless, without papers. Without remedy strong enough to keep me alive. Without being dead. But prey to the ache of hope. All the languages were spoken in the world except mine. All the books were writing to me, except the one I’d have liked to rea
hook: A story that arrives like a phone call in the dark—defenceless, without papers, without remedy. Pure atmospheric dread.

title: Who Is Antigone? The 2500-Year-Old Rebel With a Cause.
description: “Antigone” gave us the original “bad girl,” but its themes go beyond that. How do adaptations keep making Sophocles’ ideas about democracy and theater new?
hook: Antigone keeps getting remade because she refuses to die: the original bad girl whose rebellion still terrifies us.

title: B-Sides: Thomas De Quincey’s “The English Mail-Coach”
description:
“The English Mail-Coach” is one of the great meditations on mortality, on the speed with which life runs through our fingers, on how easily existence in all its radiance can be snuffed out.

The post B-Sides: Thomas De Quincey’s “The English Mail-Coach” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: De Quincey on mortality and speed: how existence in all its radiance can vanish in a blink—a haunting meditation.

title: ‘I’ve learned first-hand how evil is tolerated’: Colm Tóibín on living in the US under Trump
description:
The Brooklyn author on immigration and the inspiration behind his latest collection of stories

I often write the first paragraph of a story in a notebook, add to it every so often or leave it there to see if something might emerge from it. In 2008, in San Francisco, I went with three friends on a hike near Muir Woods overlooking the Pacific Ocean. At the summit, there was a kind of lodge where you could get a bed for the night and use the kitchen to make your own dinner. The view was sp
hook: Colm Tóibín on Trump, immigration, and where evil is tolerated—a writer processing America's turning point in real time.

title: ‘Yes’ Review: Nadav Lapid’s Fearless, Maximalist Temperature Read of Israeli Nationalism
description:
Lapid’s film locates a dire spiritual crisis facing the nation of his birth.

The post ‘Yes’ Review: Nadav Lapid’s Fearless, Maximalist Temperature Read of Israeli Nationalism appeared first on
hook: Nadav Lapid's fearless maximalism exposes the spiritual crisis at the heart of Israeli nationalism through pure cinema.

title: Planescape: Torment is the best D&D RPG of all time, and it's still waiting for a sequel
description: The beloved D&D setting of Planescape gave us exactly one game, and it's still being remembered as one of the best RPGs of all time.
hook: Planescape: Torment proved a D&D game could be literary, philosophical, and obsessively rewatchable—why hasn't it spawned a sequel?

title: Past the Firewall
description: Freedom and control on the contemporary China internet.
hook: How the contemporary China internet became a battlefield between freedom and control—a culture war with global consequences.
